# pfsense_firewall_virtual_ip (Data Source)

Retrieves a single [Firewall Virtual IP](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/firewall/virtual-ip-addresses.html) by its unique ID. Virtual IPs allow binding additional IP addresses to interfaces for use with NAT, CARP high availability, or other services.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "pfsense_firewall_virtual_ip" "example" {
  unique_id = "6718e2a5d88f2"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `unique_id` (String) Unique identifier assigned by pfSense.

### Read-Only

- `advbase` (Number) Advertisement frequency in seconds (CARP only), between 1 and 254.
- `advskew` (Number) Advertisement skew (CARP only), between 0 and 254.
- `description` (String) For administrative reference (not parsed).
- `interface` (String) Network interface for the virtual IP (e.g. 'wan', 'lan', 'opt1').
- `mode` (String) Virtual IP mode. Must be one of: `ipalias`, `carp`, `proxyarp`, `other`.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) VHID group password (CARP only).
- `subnet` (String) IP address for the virtual IP (e.g. '10.0.1.100').
- `subnet_bits` (Number) CIDR prefix length (e.g. 32 for a single host, 24 for a /24 subnet).
- `vhid` (Number) VHID group number (CARP only), between 1 and 255.
